column1: # Round 9 — Bucket 6 deep-fill: KG / vector / enterprise / research
# fallback_chain: per-record column-specific citation map -> per-record default URL (record.url)

column7: new_value
new_value
1.75 trillion searches/year served at <50ms p95 median; 18,000+ businesses globally (vendor key-figures).
Clarivate Web of Science: searches across 200M+ records returned in <2s typical (vendor product pages); no public benchmark.
Coveo Relevance Cloud reports 30%+ relevance lift over baseline on customer A/B tests; ML re-ranking via 25+ models (no public benchmark).
Glean reports ~10× faster employee answer-finding vs traditional enterprise search (customer surveys); no public benchmark.
Meilisearch median query latency <50ms on datasets up to 10M docs; benchmarks vs Algolia/Elastic published by community.
IDC MarketScape Leader 2025; no public ANN-Benchmarks or open performance numbers.
SearchUnify (a Grazitti Interactive product) — privately held; funding undisclosed; ~200 staff per LinkedIn.
column10: SearchUnify owned by Grazitti Interactive — private, funding undisclosed

column7: No public benchmark; vendor cites customer case studies (51-200-person enterprise support teams).
SPARK Matrix Leader Q4 2025; Gartner-cited for AI assistants. No public neutral benchmark.
No public ANN-Benchmarks entry; vendor publishes per-customer case-study throughput numbers (e.g. ~1B+ triples on commodity hardware).
Vendor reports up to 80× faster vs Neptune Database on graph-analytic workloads (PageRank, centrality); no neutral benchmark.
No public ANN-Benchmarks or LDBC entry; performance inherits PostgreSQL planner — competitive on small graphs (<10M edges), degrades on deep traversals.
Multi-model benchmark vs Neo4j, MongoDB, PostgreSQL on NoSQL workloads — ArangoDB reports parity-to-better on shortest-path and aggregation queries (ArangoDB benchmark series 2018–2023).
Vendor benchmark vs Neo4j on 1B-edge social graph: Dgraph reports 2-5× faster reads, 5-10× faster writes at 8-node cluster scale (Dgraph 2018 benchmark; no recent neutral repeat).
Diffbot Knowledge Graph reports 10B+ entities and 1T+ facts; query latency 50-200ms on entity lookups (vendor docs).
GraphRAG-Bench leader (Dec 2024): Hit@1 76.7%, Hit@5 89.5%, sub-140ms multi-hop retrieval on Llama-3.1-70B — outperforming Neo4j, ArangoDB, Memgraph.
LDBC Snapchat benchmark: Kuzu outperforms DuckDB-style relational by 2-5× on graph patterns; embedded analytical-graph design optimised for joins.

Vendor benchmark vs Neo4j: 120× faster on shortest-path, 7× faster on PageRank, ~6× faster on triangle counting (Memgraph internal Mage benchmark, 2024); in-memory architecture is the source of the speed-up.
Vendor benchmark: 100× faster than competing graph DBs on path queries at 100B+ vertex scale (Meituan production deployment).
500k+ queries/sec on standard hardware; vendor-reported sub-second multi-hop traversals over billions of relationships (Neo4j benchmark suite); no published ANN-Benchmarks entry for vector add-on.
No public ANN-Benchmarks entry; vendor publishes Berlin SPARQL Benchmark and W3C BSBM 100M-triple numbers.
No public benchmark; Palantir publishes customer case studies (Airbus, Merck) describing 10× faster decision cycles.
No public benchmark; vendor publishes customer KPI case studies (Boehringer Ingelheim, EFSA).
No public ANN-Benchmarks entry; vendor reports 95% accuracy on knowledge-grounded answers via Voicebox + Knowledge Catalog on customer ontologies.
LDBC Social Network Benchmark winner (2018): up to 245× faster than peers on 30-hop deep-link analytics; vendor still cites LDBC SF-10000 leadership in 2024 marketing.
No public ANN-Benchmarks entry; vendor publishes SPARQL throughput on customer ontologies (typically 50M-500M triples).
LongMemEval: reports 60.6% overall accuracy with GPT-4o-mini (atomic-note + Zettelkasten linking); LoCoMo ablations show consistent gains.
LongMemEval: ~62% overall accuracy with beacon_ratio=16; up to 1M-token contexts; 30× prefill speed-up via memory beacons.
LongMemEval: 80%+ accuracy with GPT-4o-mini; MemCubes outperform vanilla RAG by 20-30% on LoCoMo (vendor benchmark v2.0).
Titans / Atlas / MIRAS family: scales to 2M+ tokens; outperforms Transformers + linear-attention baselines on BABILong, NIAH, and language-modelling perplexity.
Deep Lake reports +22% retrieval accuracy vs Pinecone/Weaviate on enterprise RAG workloads; 80% cheaper cost claim.
No public ANN-Benchmarks entry; designed for ease-of-use (LangChain) not raw throughput; HNSW under the hood.
Databricks reports 7× lower TCO and faster index builds vs comparable managed vector services (2026 blog).
Elastic 8.12 + RaBitQ binary quantisation: 32× memory reduction, recall@10 >0.95 on MS MARCO; OpenSearch publishes similar gains.
LanceDB benchmark: 100× faster than Parquet, 2-5× faster than Pinecone on cold-start queries; columnar lakehouse design.
No public ANN-Benchmarks entry; Marqo emphasises end-to-end multimodal pipeline (embed + index + serve) over raw vector throughput.
VectorDBBench: Milvus is in top tier for 768d-OpenAI-1M (95% recall, ~5k QPS); supports GPU index (RAFT) for 10× throughput.
No public ANN-Benchmarks entry; vendor reports parity with dedicated vector DBs at <10M vectors, with converged document+vector advantage.
ANN-Benchmarks: pgvector HNSW achieves ~1k QPS at recall 0.95 on SIFT-1M; competitive with dedicated vector DBs for <10M vectors.
p95 query latency <50ms at 100M-vector scale; ANN-Benchmarks-equivalent recall@10 >0.95 on most workloads (vendor benchmark).
Qdrant publishes ANN-Benchmarks: 4-10× faster than competitors at equivalent recall (Qdrant 2024 benchmark).
Cortex Search: 12%+ retrieval lift over pure vector via hybrid lexical+semantic ranking (vendor blog).
Turbopuffer benchmark: 100M-vector queries at <100ms p95 from object storage (S3-backed serverless design).
Billion-scale ANN benchmark: Vespa serves 1B vectors at <100ms p95 from disk (HNSW + binary quantisation); powers Spotify/Yahoo/Perplexity.
ANN-Benchmarks: 13k QPS at recall 0.95 on SIFT-1M (HNSW, single node); 1B-vector cluster benchmark — 100ms p95.

Native graph-memory + vector search managed by AWS.
Apache 2.0.
Rated #1 graph DB Fall 2025. Open-source + ArangoGraph cloud.
Powers product-recommendation knowledge graphs with LLM tool-call annotations.
#1 on GraphRAG-Bench. Sub-140ms multi-hop retrieval. AGPLv3 + cloud.
Sub-3ms recall via MCP.
Used by NASA and Cedars-Sinai for GenAI knowledge graphs.
100× faster path queries. Open-source + commercial.
EMNLP 2025 Oral. +48.36% F1 and +46.18% BLEU-1 over baselines on LoCoMo with GPT-4o-mini. 217★.
Reports 83.0% overall accuracy on LongMemEval.
58.3% overall accuracy on LoCoMo (claims SOTA across five reasoning categories).
Beats Zep by ~9.0% accuracy and ~9.7% recall on LongMemEval with Llama-3.1-70B. 73.8% accuracy / 76.6% recall on LongMemEval with GPT-4o-mini. 10–40% lower latency than Mem0/Zep/A-MEM/LoCoMo-RAG.
Up to 1M-token contexts (with beacon_ratio=16). 30× pre-fill speedup via caching. Encoding+chunking+indexing 200K-token context: ~35s cold, 1.5s from cache.
7.9k★. v2.0 "Stardust" Dec 2025.
Reports 96.6% Recall@5 on LongMemEval (later updated to 98.4% on held-out data after benchmark-tuning controversy). 47k★ in two weeks at launch.
75.30% on LoCoMo, 76.88% on LongMemEval-S (GPT-4o-mini). Reduces recalled context by ~52.20% on LoCoMo via recall planning + gating.
Scales to 2M+ tokens. Reports outperforming Transformers + recent linear-recurrent models on language modeling, common-sense reasoning, genomics, and time series.
+22% retrieval accuracy claim. Reports 80% cheaper than comparable vector DBs.
7× lower cost (2026). Memory-scaling docs address multi-user agent memory patterns.

Converged document + vector store for agentic RAG.
PostgreSQL license. Most-deployed open-source vector store.
12%+ retrieval lift over pure vector.
